growth_records.medium: Minimized culture medium (Silva et al.) with fructose

sec.method: continuously produced; culture harvested at 1.2 L/day; no per-biomass secretion rate reported

growth_records.cite: Tavares, João et al. New Insights on Gordonia alkanivorans Strain 1B Surface-Active Biomolecules: Gordofactin Properties. Molecules (2024). DOI:10.3390/molecules30010001. [PMC11720751]
growth_records.snippet: Thus, the bacterial biosurfactant was produced within a 3.3 L bioreactor (Bioflo III...), in continuous culture, with a 0.65-0.70 L working volume, a dilution rate of 0.0675 ± 0.0015 h−1, an aeration rate of 2 vvm... temperature controlled
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Actinobacteria/Gordonia) steady-state chemostat. At steady state mu = D = 0.0675 h-1 (reported dilution rate). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0675 = 10.27 h. Steady-state biomass X = 8.42 g/L; feed fructose S0 = 20 g/L. Yxs derived assuming near-complete carbon-limited fructose consumption (residual fructose NOT reported): Yxs = X/S0 = 8.42/20 = 0.421 g DW/g fructose. Fructose uptake qS derived from chemostat balance assuming full consumption: qS = D*S0/X = 0.0675*20/8.42 = 0.1603 g fructose/gDW/h; /180.16 g/mol *1000 = 0.890 mmol/gDW/h — flagged is_experimental:false because residual fructose was not measured (assumption of complete consumption). Gordofactin is an extracellular biosurfactant, not a central-metabolism exchange product; no quantitative secretion rate given (only volumetric harvest of 1.2 L/day).

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 10586 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/10586
